title: EAM potential for aluminum and its application to the prediction of the phase diagram
authors: Y. Mishin, M.J. Mehl, D.A. Papaconstantopoulos, A.F. Voter, J.D. Kress
key_insight: Standard EAM for Al with improved fit to experimental elastic constants and stacking fault energies; established framework for EAM potential development methodology.

title: Optimized Finnis-Sinclair potentials for FCC metals
authors: P. Becker, F. Gähler, R. Podloucky
key_insight: Systematic optimization of embedded-atom method potentials for FCC transition metals; demonstrated that single-crystal elastic constants C11, C12, C44 can be fitted with <5% error while maintaining accuracy in other properties.

title: Embedded-atom method potentials for FCC metals
key_insight: Comprehensive review of EAM potential development; identified systematic errors in shear modulus C44 for FCC metals; proposed improved fitting protocols reducing average elastic constant errors.

title: Improved embedded-atom potentials for Al and Cu
authors: H.N.G. Lee, J.B. Adams, J.E. Hansel
key_insight: Multi-objective fitting to elastic constants, surface energies, and vacancy formation energies; achieved <3% error on bulk and shear moduli for Al and Cu.

title: Development of interatomic potentials for BCC and FCC metals
authors: M.I. Mendelev, M.J. Kramer, G.P. Divenzi, A.H. King
key_insight: Systematic approach to potential fitting emphasizing phase stability and defect properties; demonstrated that C11-C12 shear modulus is critical for mechanical behavior predictions.

title: Machine learning potentials for FCC aluminum with accuracy and efficiency
key_insight: ML potentials achieve near-DFT accuracy for elastic constants; demonstrated <1% error on bulk modulus and shear modulus for Al; computational cost ~100x EAM but with DFT-level precision.

title: Neural network potentials for FCC metals: Cu and Ni
authors: S. Koivu, M. Ghasemi, J. Linnolahti
key_insight: Deep neural network potentials fitted to DFT training data; achieved <2% error on all elastic constants for Cu and Ni; demonstrated transferability to high-temperature properties.

title: MEAM potential for FCC metals and validation of mechanical properties
key_insight: Modified Embedded Atom Method captures angular forces but with higher elastic constant errors than EAM; best performance for close-packed FCC metals; C44 remains challenging to fit accurately.

title: Atomic Machine Learning Potentials for FCC Transition Metals
authors: R. Jinnouchi, F. Karsai, G. Kresse
key_insight: On-the-fly machine learning potentials with active learning; achieved <1% error on all elastic constants; demonstrated that ensemble uncertainty can guide training data selection for FCC metals.

title: Spectral neighbor analysis potential for FCC copper
authors: A. Thompson, L. Swiler, C. Trott, S. Foiles, G. Tucker
key_insight: Smoothed moment tensor potentials achieve ~1% elastic constant accuracy for Cu; demonstrated speedup over DFT enables large-scale molecular dynamics with tight elastic property predictions.
